title: TC-SWUPDATE-LEGEND50-001: Verify 'Can't Be Updated' message visibility for Voyager Legend 50 on outdated firmware
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 headset with outdated firmware version (e.g., v1.2.0 where latest is v2.0.0) is paired and connected to phone.
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Observe the Software/Update screen content.
Verify the 'Can't Be Updated' message is displayed.
Verify NO update button or 'Update Now' / 'Check for Updates' button is present.
Verify the message text clearly indicates the charge case firmware cannot be updated via the app.
expected: The Software screen displays the 'Can't Be Updated' message. No update button is rendered. The message text explains that the Voyager Legend 50 charge case firmware cannot be updated through the Poly Studio Mobile app. Actual (If Bug): App shows a generic error or empty state without the expected informational message, or incorrectly shows an update button that fails.

title: TC-SWUPDATE-LEGEND50-002: Verify 'Can't Be Updated' persists after app restart
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 with outdated firmware connected.
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Confirm the 'Can't Be Updated' message is displayed.
Press Home button to background the app.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app from recent apps.
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Observe the Software screen.
expected: The 'Can't Be Updated' message persists after app backgrounding and reopening. No update button appears. The message content is identical to the pre-background state. Actual (If Bug): After reopening, the app briefly shows a loading state then incorrectly displays an update button, or the message disappears.

title: TC-SWUPDATE-LEGEND50-003: Verify 'Can't Be Updated' persists after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 with outdated firmware connected.
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Confirm the 'Can't Be Updated' message is displayed.
Disconnect Bluetooth from phone settings or app.
Wait 10 seconds.
Reconnect Bluetooth to the Voyager Legend 50.
Wait for app to re-establish connection.
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Observe the Software screen.
expected: After Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect cycle, the 'Can't Be Updated' message is still displayed. No update button appears. The app correctly recognizes the device and maintains the informational message. Actual (If Bug): After reconnection, the app attempts to fetch update status and shows a spinner indefinitely, or incorrectly shows an update button that leads to an error.

title: TC-SWUPDATE-LEGEND50-004: Verify 'Can't Be Updated' persists after app force-kill and relaunch
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 with outdated firmware connected.
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Confirm the 'Can't Be Updated' message is displayed.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recent apps).
Wait 5 seconds.
Relaunch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for device connection to restore.
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Observe the Software screen.
expected: After app force-kill and relaunch, the 'Can't Be Updated' message is displayed correctly. No update button appears. The app does not show any transient error or loading failure. Actual (If Bug): After relaunch, the app shows a generic 'Unable to load' error or the message is missing, requiring the user to navigate away and back.

title: TC-SWUPDATE-LEGEND50-005: Verify 'Can't Be Updated' message for Voyager Legend 50 vs other devices (Device Asymmetry)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 with outdated firmware connected. Also have a Poly Voyager Free 60+ (or other updatable device) available.
Connect Voyager Legend 50. Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Verify 'Can't Be Updated' message is displayed and NO update button.
Disconnect Voyager Legend 50.
Connect the updatable device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+).
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Verify that an update button IS displayed and the 'Can't Be Updated' message is NOT shown.
Disconnect updatable device and reconnect Voyager Legend 50.
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Verify the 'Can't Be Updated' message returns.
expected: Device asymmetry is correctly handled: Voyager Legend 50 shows 'Can't Be Updated' with no update button, while updatable devices show an update button. Switching back to Legend 50 restores the informational message. Actual (If Bug): The app caches the updatable device's state and incorrectly shows an update button for Legend 50, or shows the 'Can't Be Updated' message on an updatable device.

title: TC-SWUPDATE-LEGEND50-006: Verify 'Can't Be Updated' message during firmware update of another device
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 with outdated firmware connected. A second updatable device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+) is also paired but not connected.
Connect Voyager Legend 50.
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Verify 'Can't Be Updated' message is displayed.
While staying on the Software screen, connect the updatable device via Bluetooth (multi-device scenario).
Observe the Software screen behavior.
Switch back to Voyager Legend 50 connection.
Observe the Software screen.
expected: The 'Can't Be Updated' message remains stable on the Software screen for Legend 50 even when another device connects/disconnects in the background. No update button appears for Legend 50 at any point. Actual (If Bug): Connecting an updatable device causes the Legend 50 screen to refresh and incorrectly show update options, or the app crashes due to device state conflict.

title: TC-SWUPDATE-LEGEND50-007: Verify 'Can't Be Updated' message on phone reboot
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 with outdated firmware connected and 'Can't Be Updated' message confirmed visible.
Reboot the phone.
After phone restart, launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for automatic reconnection to Voyager Legend 50.
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Observe the Software screen.
expected: After phone reboot, the 'Can't Be Updated' message is displayed correctly. No update button appears. The app correctly identifies the device model and firmware version after reboot and shows the appropriate informational message. Actual (If Bug): After reboot, the app shows a blank screen, loading spinner that never resolves, or incorrectly attempts to fetch an update and shows an error.

title: TC-SWUPDATE-LEGEND50-008: Verify 'Can't Be Updated' message with outdated firmware on first-ever connect
steps: Precondition: Voyager Legend 50 with outdated firmware has never been connected to this phone before. Factory reset the headset if possible.
Pair and connect Voyager Legend 50 to the phone for the first time.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and complete initial setup/onboarding.
Navigate to: Device → Device Settings → Software
Observe the Software screen on first connection.
expected: On first-ever connection, the Software screen displays the 'Can't Be Updated' message for Voyager Legend 50. No update button is shown. The app does not attempt to download or stage a firmware update. Actual (If Bug): The app shows a 'Checking for updates' spinner indefinitely on first connect, or incorrectly prompts to update firmware that cannot be updated.
